programs.name: Medicaid Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay for Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and co-pays; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors on Medicare; specific income limits (e.g., QMB ~100% FPL, SLMB ~120% FPL, QI ~135% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Premium payments up to $185/mo Part B (2026 est.); full cost-sharing coverage for QMB.

programs.name: Aged and Disabled Waiver
programs.what_it_does: This home and community-based services (HCBS) waiver provides long-term care services in the home or community to prevent or delay nursing home placement.[1][5] It includes personal care, adult day care, and other supports for frail seniors.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals meeting medical and financial criteria (income ≤$2,982/mo, assets ≤$2,000).[1][5]
programs.benefit_value: Alternative to nursing home care; same financial limits as Institutional Medicaid ($2,982/mo income, $2,000 assets).[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and hospitalization if needed. It is a Medicare and Medicaid program designed to help participants avoid nursing homes.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated coverage of Medicare and Medicaid services; value varies by location (limited availability in WV).

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy nutritious food. Administered through West Virginia PATH for eligible low-income households.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, disabled, and families; seniors may qualify with higher income disregards.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/mo per household (varies by income/size); max ~$291/mo for one person (2026 est.).

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP helps low-income households with heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. West Virginia administers federal funds for energy costs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households, prioritizing seniors, disabled, and families with young children.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-1,000+ depending on fuel type, income, and need.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs for low-income homes. Reduces energy costs and improves health/safety.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households, with priority for seniors, disabled, and children.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements; average savings $300+/year on energy bills.

programs.name: SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP provides free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Part D, and supplemental plans. West Virginia's program helps seniors choose and understand options.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries, caregivers, and families seeking insurance guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save thousands on premiums/drugs annually.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Home Delivered Meals)
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors. Often funded through Area Agencies on Aging in West Virginia.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week; ~$8-10/meal value, often low/no cost based on income.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program (NFCSP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, and support for family caregivers of seniors. Administered via West Virginia Bureau for Senior Services.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of adults 60+ or grandparents raising grandchildren.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,500+/year; training and counseling services.

programs.name: SCSEP (Senior Community Service Employment Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training for low-income seniors to gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work ~20 hrs/wk; wage subsidy up to $10-15/hr.

programs.name: Legal Aid West Virginia (Senior Legal Services)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for low-income seniors on issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. Funded partly through Older Americans Act.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation; pro bono services worth thousands.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. West Virginia program protects nursing home/assisted living residents.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy and complaint resolution services.

programs.name: Lighthouse Program
programs.what_it_does: The Lighthouse Program assists seniors with functional needs in their homes who are disqualified from Medicaid due to income or assets. It provides state-funded support in personal care, mobility, nutrition, and housekeeping, available in every county.[1][2][7]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with functional needs in their homes whose income or assets disqualify them for Medicaid services; must be West Virginia residents.[1][2]
programs.whats_unique: Funded entirely by state monies, not federal or Medicaid, filling the gap for non-Medicaid-eligible seniors needing in-home support; not a standard federal program and available county-wide in WV.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Support in four areas: personal care, mobility, nutrition, and housekeeping; specific dollar amounts not detailed in sources.

programs.name: Take Me Home Program
programs.what_it_does: The Take Me Home program helps residents of long-term care facilities transition back to community living by addressing barriers like deposits, household items, and supports. It offers transition coordination, pre-transition case management, and community transition services to qualified applicants.[1][6]
programs.who_its_for: Qualified residents of long-term care facilities in West Virginia wishing to return home.[1][6]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific program overcoming transition obstacles with targeted services like security deposits and furniture; not a universal federal offering.[1][6]
programs.benefit_value: Services including transition coordination, case management, and community supports such as deposits and household items; no fixed dollar value specified.

programs.name: Visually Impaired Seniors In-Home Outreach and Networking Services (VISIONS)
programs.what_it_does: VISIONS is an independent living program providing free services to seniors with vision loss directly in their homes or communities. Services support maintaining independence without requiring relocation.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Qualified seniors with vision loss in West Virginia.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-operated in-home program exclusively for visually impaired seniors, delivered free without need to leave home; not a standard federal senior benefit.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free independent living services; specific value not quantified.

programs.name: WV Bureau of Senior Services Home Modification and Accessibility Grant Program (Aging Well, Safe at Home Initiative)
programs.what_it_does: This program provides grants for home modifications and accessibility devices to help seniors live more independently at home. It targets adults age 60+ demonstrating a need for such improvements.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Adults age 60+ in West Virginia who can demonstrate need for home modifications or accessibility devices.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded grant program specifically for senior home accessibility in WV, beyond federal standards; focuses on independent living enhancements.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Grants for modifications and devices; exact amounts vary by need and not specified.
